title: Memory Addressing: Types, Byte Order and Decoding
summary: Covers absolute, relative and symbolic addresses, big-endian vs. little-endian byte order, and full vs. partial address decoding.
content_points: Absolute address: assigned location number (from 0). Relative: offset from a base. Symbolic: named label (variable).
Big endian: most significant byte at lowest address. Little endian: least significant byte at lowest address.
Intel x86 is little-endian. Example: 0xB67A49E3 at 150–153: 150→E3, 151→49, 152→7A, 153→B6.
Full decoding: each address uniquely selects one circuit. Partial: some address bits ignored → aliasing.
learning_objectives: Distinguish absolute, relative, and symbolic addresses
Explain big-endian and little-endian byte ordering
Show how a multi-byte value is stored in each ordering
